title: Keynote: The Death of Trust in Digital Systems
description: A deep dive into the systemic vulnerabilities at the heart of every trust-based digital system we rely on — from PKI to zero-knowledge proofs. Raven walks through recent real-world collapses of digital trust and what it means for the future of encryption.

title: Reverse Engineering Proprietary Protocols
description: Hands-on techniques for protocol recovery, including side-channel analysis, bus snooping, and fuzzing strategies that reveal hidden communication channels in supposedly closed-source hardware. Covers IoT gadgets, medical devices, and automotive ECUs.

title: Building Your Own SDR from Scratch
description: A hands-on deep dive into the design, fabrication, and programming of custom SDR hardware. Learn about ADC/DAC selection, FPGA pipeline design, GNU Radio integration, and the art of squeezing maximum performance out of commodity silicon.

title: IoT Security: From Firmware to Flame
description: Covers the full attack surface of IoT devices — from physical port enumeration and chip-off forensic extraction to dynamic malware analysis in emulated firmware environments. Discusses the growing threat of firmware supply chain attacks.

title: OSINT at Scale: Mapping the Shadow Network
Social Engineering: The Human Firewall
description: Covers the full spectrum of social engineering techniques, from pre-texting and pretext development to advanced impersonation strategies. Discusses the ethical boundaries of social engineering research and how to use these techniques defensively.

title: Panel: Regulation vs. Innovation in Security Research
After Hours: Rooftop Social Engineering Range
Keynote: AI-Powered Attacks and How to Defend
description: Explores the dual-use nature of AI — how the same techniques that power breakthrough research can also be weaponized by adversaries. Walks through recent attack demonstrations, discusses the current state of AI defense research, and presents a roadmap for building AI systems that are secure by design.

title: Exploit Development: Bypassing Modern Mitigations
description: Walks through recent exploit chains that bypass modern mitigations, with deep dives into kernel exploitation techniques, user-space ROP, and the emerging role of AI-assisted vulnerability discovery. Covers ASLR, DEP, CFG, CET bypasses on Windows, Linux, and macOS.

title: LoRa and Long-Range Wireless Exploitation
description: Walks through real attack demonstrations on LoRa, Sigfox, and NB-IoT deployments — from jamming and replay attacks to full protocol exploitation. Discusses the emerging defenses being built into next-generation LPWAN stacks.

title: Supply Chain Attacks: When Your Tools Turn
Steganography: Hiding Data in Plain Sight
description: Covers the latest advances in steganographic techniques, from LSB embedding and DCT-based methods to deep learning-driven approaches. Demonstrates practical tools and walks through real-world use cases, including secure whistleblowing channels and covert data exfiltration.
